mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting both standalone and clustered configurations. It includes features like monitoring, failover, and security options, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository for the Redis Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Redis Operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Redis Operator.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Redis Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all pods in the redis-operator namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Redis cluster using a Custom Resource (CR).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  version: "6.2.6"
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  version: "6.2.6"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Redis cluster is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all Redis instances in the redis-operator namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Redis instances managed by the operator, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Backup existing Redis configurations.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Redis Operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Redis configuration is incorrect or if there are issues with the underlying storage.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redis-operator' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Redis instances not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the network policies and ensure that the Redis service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis version not supported'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Redis version in the Custom Resource may not be compatible with the operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Redis version specified is >=6.x and supported by the operator.

**Affected versions:** <= 0.19

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 6.2.x, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.18.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see the Redis instances listed with 'kubectl get redis -n redis-operator' showing their status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  version: "6.2.6"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
